attacking_style: Uses climbing ability to bridge across to leaders rather than launching pure attacks. Shows initiative to move solo when opportunities arise, but struggles to convert strong positioning into wins.
team_tactics: Limited tactical support evident; relies on individual positioning skill and reading the race rather than team-led setups. Development through Contador program suggests focus on opportunistic aggression.
terrain_preference: Climbing terrain where ability to solo across gaps becomes an asset. Suited to selective, attritional racing rather than explosive summit finishes.
vulnerability: Lack of winning punch in final moments despite achieving strong positioning. Converts tactical nous into podium placings rather than victories, suggesting finishing speed deficit.
key_patterns: Strong positioning skill gets into winning moves but lacks killer instinct (2 observations)
Uses solo bridging efforts via climbing strength rather than following wheels (2 observations)
Converts good race-reading into podiums but not wins, second place twice consecutively (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: showed initiative and climbing strength but repeated second places suggest hitting his ceiling rather than improving
